commit.message:
Fix token count fallbacks and reset on restart

Fall back to estimated per-entry counts when the real tokenizer call fails, and clear Health chat token UI state when restarting a session after context-window recovery fails.

Fix token usage UI duplication and counting overhead

Extract a shared TokenUsageBar, reuse a single SystemLanguageModel in ChatViewModel, and avoid hammering tokenUsage() while the latest entry is streaming. Also restore tool-call message formatting and make HealthChatViewModel token counting consistent.

Fix token budget window order and display token usage (#102)

Co-authored-by: Cursor Agent <cursoragent@cursor.com>

commit.message:
Clean up Transcript+TokenCounting after bot optimizations

- Extract estimatedTokenBudgetWindow and realTokenBudgetWindow into
  private helpers for readability
- Fix entry ordering in estimated window (was appending reversed)
- Simplify estimation utilities (use division instead of multiplication)
- Remove unnecessary .reversed() in binary search candidates
- Zero SwiftLint violations

Optimize token budget windowing and remove dead APIs (#101)

Co-authored-by: Cursor Agent <cursoragent@cursor.com>

commit.message:
Fix per-entry token count not updating after streaming completes

The .task(id: entry.id) only fired once since the entry ID is stable
during streaming. Use a content-based hash as the task trigger so the
token count recalculates when the entry text changes.

Show token count in ResultDisplay across all example views

Add optional tokenCount badge to ResultDisplay component and pass
executor.lastTokenCount from all example views that use it.

Add real token usage tracking using SystemLanguageModel.TokenUsage API

Integrate the new TokenUsage API from iOS 26.4 / Xcode 26.4 (Swift 6.3)
across the app, gated with #if compiler(>=6.3) and @available checks.
Falls back to the existing character-based estimation on older versions.

Core:
- Transcript+TokenCounting: Add realTokenCount(), unified tokenCount(),
  and async versions of isApproachingLimit/entriesWithinTokenBudget
- AppConfiguration: Dynamic contextSize via model.contextSize (compiler-gated)

ViewModels:
- ChatViewModel: Real token counts for sliding window, expose
  currentTokenCount/maxContextSize/tokenUsageFraction for UI
- HealthChatViewModel: Same token tracking pattern
- RAGChatViewModel: Track lastTokenCount per generation
- ExampleExecutor: Track lastTokenCount after each operation

UI:
- ChatView: Color-coded token usage progress bar
- TranscriptEntryView: Per-entry token count labels
- GenerationOptionsView: Token budget display after generation

Update dependencies and add .build-sim to gitignore

- Update PicoDocs to 1.0.1 (Swift 6.3 @Sendable fix)
- Update LumoKit and VecturaKit to latest main
- Drop mlx-swift, mlx-swift-lm, swift-numerics (no longer needed)
- Add .build-sim/ to .gitignore

commit.message: Remove Trip Brief localization strings
Remove Trip Brief workflow UI
Fix health response lookup and web tool destination
Fix streaming task cancellation and audio buffer copying
Fix streaming task cancellation and audio buffer copying

Fix audio buffer corruption and implement streaming task cancellation

Bug 1: Audio buffer append moved to async context risks data corruption
- Move recognitionRequest?.append(buffer) outside the Task block to execute
  synchronously on the audio thread before the buffer's data is recycled
- Only dispatch MainActor-dependent code (processAudioBuffer) asynchronously

Bug 2: Unused streamingTask variable is dead code
- Wrap streaming loops in Task and assign to streamingTask in ChatViewModel,
  RAGChatViewModel, and HealthChatViewModel
- This enables the existing cancellation code in clearChat()/tearDown() to work

Fix architecture review issues: concurrency, error handling, accessibility, and structural improvements

Concurrency Safety:
- Add @MainActor to NavigationCoordinator and RAGChatEntry class declarations
- Fix audio tap closure to dispatch property access to MainActor
- Fix recognition task callback actor isolation
- Migrate DispatchQueue.main.async patterns to Task { @MainActor in } in SpeechSynthesizer, SpeechRecognizer, PermissionManager
- Store and cancel fire-and-forget Task in VoiceViewModel.showError()
- Add @MainActor to RAGService, mark ToolExecutor closures @MainActor

Error Handling:
- Make HealthDataManager.fetchTodayHealthData() throw with HealthDataManagerError
- Replace generic error message in HealthChatViewModel with FoundationModelsErrorHandler
- Surface RAG initialization errors to user instead of silent nil
- Fix misleading error in ChatViewModel.handleContextWindowExceeded
- Rename sessionTimeoutHours to sessionTimeout (value is TimeInterval)
- Make VoiceViewModel errors persist for permission-related failures
- Remove vestigial LanguageService.errorMessage property

Accessibility:
- Add VoiceOver support to Health Dashboard (score ring, metric cards, progress bars, activity rings, insights)
- Add accessibility to Voice interface and permission request views
- Mirror MessageBubbleView accessibility pattern in HealthMessageBubbleView
- Add accessibilityLabel to all icon-only buttons (send, voice, copy)
- Replace Font.system(size:) with text styles for Dynamic Type support
- Add .accessibilityElement(children: .combine) to GenericCardView and ToolButton

Structural:
- Extract VoiceState and SamplingStrategy from ChatViewModel to own files
- Split DataModels.swift: move example models near consuming views
- Add streaming task cancellation to ChatViewModel, HealthChatViewModel, RAGChatViewModel
- Make PermissionManager and SpeechSynthesisService injectable in ChatViewModel
- Make HealthDataManager injectable in HealthChatViewModel
- Pass ChatViewModel explicitly to ChatInputView instead of environment
Update localization strings for new UI labels

Fix concurrency safety issues and improve error handling

- SpeechRecognizer: Copy handler dictionary before iteration to prevent
  "Dictionary was modified during iteration" crash
- SpeechSynthesizer: Guard against concurrent continuation overwrites
  by checking pendingContinuation before starting new synthesis
- ChatViewModel: Add tearDown() method for proper resource cleanup
- ChatView: Call tearDown() on disappear to cancel running tasks
- HealthChatViewModel: Log errors instead of silently swallowing them

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add visually rich TripBriefResultView with sectioned cards

Replaces basic ResultDisplay with a custom view featuring:
- Weather section with orange sun icon
- Key Insights section with yellow lightbulb and colored bullet points
- Source section with blue link icon
- Parsing logic to extract sections from markdown response
- Fallback to raw text if parsing fails
- Added preview with sample result data for testing

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add placeholder values for easier testing in previews

Pre-fills destination with "Tokyo, Japan" and guide URL with Lonely Planet
link so the button is enabled by default for testing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add Trip Brief multi-tool workflow to Tools tab

Implements issue #81 by adding a lightweight "Trip Brief" workflow that
demonstrates multi-tool orchestration using Weather and Web Metadata tools.
Users can enter a destination and guide URL to generate a 3-5 bullet brief
with weather summary and citations.

- Add TripBriefWorkflowView with compact always-expanded UI
- Add workflow section above tools grid in ToolsView
- Reuse existing ToolExecutor, ToolExecuteButton, and ResultDisplay components

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Update localization keys for new UI labels

Add placeholder entries for new examples, tools, and RAG strings and refresh extraction metadata.

Remove reference to non-existent .web case in ToolExample

The ToolExample enum doesn't have a .web case, but the destination
switch was referencing it, causing a build failure.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Fix response extraction for current turn
Fix health chat session persistence and response capture

commit.message:
Escape prompts in code example builders

Centralize string escaping to safely embed prompts and instructions
across dynamic code examples.

Keep RAG sources out of system instructions

Move context into the user prompt to reduce prompt-injection risk while preserving citations.
Improve keyless Search1API error messaging

Fix journaling guides and prompts

Reorder @Guide arguments and wrap long journaling prompts to satisfy SwiftLint.
Guard RAG ask submission while working

Enhance journaling reflections output

Add uplifting messages, sentence starters, and themes to mirror Stoic-style guidance.
Update prompts, instructions, and docs to reflect the richer journaling output.

Refocus RAG example on cited doc Q&A

Highlight source-backed answers with a simple single-question flow.
Fix journaling code sample newlines

Switch web search to keyless Search1API

Use a keyless Search1API tool for web search and remove Exa key settings.

Add journaling example

Replace the business ideas example with journaling prompts and summaries.

Update localization keys for new UI labels

Add placeholder entries for new examples, tools, and RAG strings and refresh extraction metadata.

Fix Swift 6 build errors and warnings

- Remove array literal syntax in AppShortcuts for @AppShortcutsBuilder
- Add @ViewBuilder to computed properties with @Bindable declarations
- Remove nonisolated from Intent structs with @Parameter wrappers
- Add @MainActor to HealthChatViewModel per project conventions

Improve RAG chat resilience and UX

Guard against chatting without indexed content, avoid overlapping searches, and auto-scroll new responses. Also clean up linting by isolating long HealthKit service files.

Inline Voice Mode with Interruption Support

commit.message:
Fix inline voice lifecycle and controls

Ensure recognition restarts after TTS or interruptions, cancel observation tasks on cleanup, and add stop/end controls on non-iOS platforms to prevent stuck sessions.
Move Star History chart to README footer.
Add Star History chart to README.

Fix fatalError in RAGConfig.default

Replace fatalError with optional return type for safer error handling.
The ViewModel already handles nil config gracefully via existing guard checks.

Remove plan.md and fix speechRecognizer cleanup

- Remove plan.md implementation doc (shouldn't be in repo)
- Properly nil out speechRecognizer in cancelVoiceMode() to prevent memory leaks

Update plan.md as complete source of truth

- Mark all phases as DONE
- Document AsyncStream implementation
- Add voice state UI mapping table
- Include testing checklist results

Add AsyncStream stateValues for modern Swift concurrency

- stateValues: AsyncStream<SpeechRecognitionState>
- Replaces callback-based addStateChangeHandler pattern
- Enables for-await state observation in ChatViewModel

Remove voice modal sheet and add dynamic navigation title

- Removed .sheet(isPresented: $showVoiceSheet) for VoiceView
- Removed showVoiceSheet state variable
- Dynamic navigation title based on voiceState.isActive

Add inline voice UI to ChatInputView

- ProgressView + Cancel during preparing state
- End button during listening state
- Stop button during speaking state
- Partial text display while listening

Add VoiceState enum and voice methods to ChatViewModel

- VoiceState: idle, preparing, listening, processing, speaking, error
- startVoiceMode() with permission check and pre-warm
- stopVoiceModeAndSend() for multi-turn voice conversations
- stopSpeaking() to interrupt TTS
- AsyncStream observer for speech state changes
Enhance VoiceState enum with computed helpers, error case, didSet pattern
Update plan: use VoiceState enum instead of booleans

Add detailed implementation plan for inline voice mode

Plan includes:
- Voice button state machine (microphone → loading → end)
- Pre-warm Foundation Models session on tap
- Loading state with indeterminate ProgressView + Cancel
- Navigation title changes to "Voice"
- Message flow integration with transcript
- Files to modify with code examples
- Testing checklist

Reference: ChatGPT inline voice mode (Nov 2025)

Simplify RAG sample loading and remove chunkTitles complexity

- Remove chunkTitles dictionary (was unused for lookups)
- LoadSampleDocuments now continues on error instead of failing completely
- Capture first error but continue indexing remaining samples
- Simplify sourceTitlesForChunk for single-source case

Fix RAG sample loading errors and UserDefaults optimization

- Fix loadSampleDocuments to save state after each sample (avoids partial state on error)
- Add error cleanup to revert indexed data if loading fails mid-loop
- Store titles per source key + per chunk ID (chunkTitles) to avoid UserDefaults bloat
- Remove unused LumoKit import from RAGDocumentPickerView

Fix RAG initialization, document count, and database reconciliation

- Set indexedDocumentCount in init() from persisted indexedURLs
- Rename ensureInitialized() to loadFromDatabase() for clarity
- Reconcile persisted state with database on launch
- Add loadFromDatabase() call in RAGChatView onAppear
- Remove unused LumoKit and VecturaKit imports from RAGChatView

Fix RAG initialization, add RAGService, and improve document handling

- Fix RAGChatViewModel.initialize() not being called before use
- Add RAGService.swift for centralized RAG operations
- Move RAGChatViewModel to ViewModels/ folder
- Track sample document titles properly
- Fix document count to use indexedURLs.count
- Ensure files are tracked in Xcode project

Fix remaining RAG audit issues

- Add RAG to Examples grid (was excluded, making it unreachable)
- Add @MainActor to RAGChatViewModel for thread safety
- Support .md files via UTType(filenameExtension: "md")
- Update UI text to remove outdated Markdown claim

Use LumoKit for all indexing to keep BM25 in sync

- Use lumoKit.addDocuments() instead of direct vectura calls
- Use lumoKit.resetDB() for database reset
- Remove unused vectura property
- ChunkingConfig now throws, added try

Address RAG PR review feedback: error handling, UI fixes, and lint cleanup

- Add confirmation dialog for Clear All Documents action
- Fix security-scoped resource access for imported files
- Add @Observable to RAGChatEntry for streaming visibility
- Fix document count accumulation (now uses += instead of =)
- Fix error handling for vectura.reset() with do-catch
- Fix file importer failure case with error message
- Fix SwiftLint violations: multiple_closuers_with_trailing_closure
- Extract RAGChatEntry, RAGChunk, RAGMessageBubble to separate file

Address PR review feedback: error handling, streaming, and prompt fixes

- Add user-facing alerts for file import errors
- Fix silent failure in resetDatabase with proper error handling
- Implement streaming response with incremental UI updates
- Fix redundant user question in prompt (moved to instructions only)
- Improve document source label from "Document" to "Source"
- Refactor RAGChatEntry to class for mutable streaming content

Implement RAG example using LumoKit with proper document indexing and retrieval

- Uses LumoKit's parseAndIndex for document parsing via PicoDocs
- Implements semantic search for retrieval from indexed documents
- Handles VecturaConfig throwing initializer gracefully
- Adds macOS compatibility with #if os(iOS) guards
- Includes document picker UI for importing files (PDF, text, HTML)

readme_html:
Foundation Models Framework Example

Exploring Foundation Models

This project includes playground examples organized by chapters to help you learn everything about Apple's Foundation Models framework.

It is part of the Exploring Foundation Models book.

Requirements

  • iOS 26.0+ or macOS 26.0+ (Xcode 26.0+)
  • Xcode 26 official is required
  • Apple Intelligence enabled
  • Compatible Apple device with Apple Silicon

Try it on TestFlight

You can now try Foundation Lab on TestFlight! Join the beta: https://testflight.apple.com/join/JWR9FpP3

Getting Started

  • Clone the repository
  • Open FoundationLab.xcodeproj in Xcode
  • Ensure you have a device with Apple Intelligence enabled
  • Build and run the project
  • Explore the different capabilities through the examples!

What's Inside

The app has four main sections:

Chat

Multi-turn conversations with context management, streaming responses, and a feedback system. Includes automatic context window management with session summarization when needed.

Tools

Nine system integration tools that extend the model's capabilities:

  • Weather - Current weather for any location (OpenMeteo API)
  • Web Search - Keyless Search1API (free, limited)
  • Contacts - Search and access system contacts
  • Calendar - Create and manage calendar events
  • Reminders - AI-assisted reminder creation with priority levels
  • Location - Current location and geocoding
  • Health - HealthKit integration for health data queries
  • Music - Apple Music search (requires subscription)
  • Web Metadata - Extract metadata and generate social media summaries

Voice Interface

Talk to the model using speech:

  • Voice-to-text with real-time transcription
  • Text-to-speech responses
  • Create reminders by voice
  • Audio-reactive visualization
  • Handles all permissions automatically

Health Dashboard

AI-powered health tracking with HealthKit:

  • Personal health coach with contextual insights
  • Trend analysis and correlations
  • Predictive analytics
  • Weekly summaries and personalized health plans
  • Multiple health metrics tracking

Integrations Hub

Three sections for exploring advanced features:

  • Tools - All nine system integration examples
  • Schemas - Dynamic schema examples from basic to expert level
  • Languages - Multilingual features and language detection

Examples

Ten different example types showing framework capabilities:

  • One-shot prompts
  • Journaling
  • Creative writing
  • Structured data generation
  • Streaming responses
  • Model availability checking
  • Generation guides
  • Generation options (temperature, tokens, fitness)
  • Health dashboard
  • RAG chat with document indexing and search

Features

Core Capabilities

  • Chat: Multi-turn conversations with context management
  • Streaming: Real-time response streaming
  • Structured Generation: Type-safe data with @Generable
  • Generation Guides: Constrained outputs with @Guide
  • Tool Calling: System integrations for extended functionality
  • RAG: Document indexing and semantic search with LumoKit/VecturaKit
  • Voice: Speech-to-text and text-to-speech
  • Health: HealthKit integration with AI insights
  • Multilingual: Works in 10 languages (English, German, Spanish, French, Italian, Japanese, Korean, Portuguese, Chinese)

Dynamic Schemas

The app includes 11 dynamic schema examples ranging from basic to expert:

  • Basic schemas
  • Arrays and collections
  • Enums and union types
  • Nested objects
  • Schema references
  • Form builders
  • Invoice processing
  • Error handling patterns

Playground Examples

Four chapters with hands-on examples:

  • Chapter 2: Getting Started with Sessions (16 examples)
  • Chapter 3: Generation Options and Sampling Control (5 examples)
  • Chapter 8: Basic Tool Use (9 examples)
  • Chapter 13: Languages and Internationalization (7 examples)

Run these directly in Xcode using the #Playground directive.

Usage Examples

Basic Chat

let session = LanguageModelSession()
let response = try await session.respond(
    to: "Suggest a catchy name for a new coffee shop."
)
print(response.content)

Structured Data Generation

let session = LanguageModelSession()
let bookInfo = try await session.respond(
    to: "Suggest a sci-fi book.",
    generating: BookRecommendation.self
)
print("Title: \(bookInfo.content.title)")
print("Author: \(bookInfo.content.author)")

Tool Calling

// Single tool
let weatherSession = LanguageModelSession(tools: [WeatherTool()])
let response = try await weatherSession.respond(
    to: "Is it hotter in New Delhi or Cupertino?"
)

// Multiple tools
let multiSession = LanguageModelSession(tools: [
    WeatherTool(),
    Search1WebSearchTool(),
    ContactsTool()
])
let multiResponse = try await multiSession.respond(
    to: "Check the weather, search the web, and find my friend John's contact"
)

Streaming Responses

let session = LanguageModelSession()
let stream = session.streamResponse(to: "Write a short poem about technology.")

for try await partialText in stream {
    print("Partial: \(partialText)")
}

Voice Interface

// Speech recognition
let recognizer = SpeechRecognizer()
try recognizer.startRecognition()

// Text-to-speech
try await SpeechSynthesizer.shared.synthesizeAndSpeak(text: "Hello, how can I help you?")

Health Data

let session = LanguageModelSession(tools: [HealthDataTool()])
let response = try await session.respond(
    to: "Show me my step count trends this week"
)

Data Models

The app includes various @Generable data models for different use cases:

General Purpose

@Generable
struct BookRecommendation {
    @Guide(description: "The title of the book")
    let title: String

    @Guide(description: "The author's name")
    let author: String

    @Guide(description: "Genre of the book")
    let genre: Genre
}

@Generable
struct ProductReview {
    @Guide(description: "Product name")
    let productName: String

    @Guide(description: "Rating from 1 to 5")
    let rating: Int

    @Guide(description: "Key pros and cons")
    let pros: [String]
    let cons: [String]
}

@Generable
struct StoryOutline {
    let title: String
    let protagonist: String
    let conflict: String
    let setting: String
    let genre: StoryGenre
    let themes: [String]
}

@Generable
struct JournalEntrySummary {
    let prompt: String
    let upliftingMessage: String
    let sentenceStarters: [String]
    let summaryBullets: [String]
    let themes: [String]
}

Health Models

@Generable
struct HealthAI {
    let greeting: String
    let mood: HealthAIMood
    let motivationalMessage: String
    let focusMetrics: [String]
    let suggestions: [String]
}

@Generable
struct HealthAnalysis {
    let healthScore: Int
    let trends: HealthTrends
    let insights: [HealthInsightDetail]
    let correlations: [MetricCorrelation]
    let predictions: [HealthPrediction]
    let recommendations: [String]
}

@Generable
struct PersonalizedHealthPlan {
    let title: String
    let overview: String
    let currentStatus: String
    let weeklyActivities: [String]
    let nutritionGuidelines: NutritionPlan
    let sleepStrategy: String
    let milestones: [String]
}

Chat Context

@Generable
struct ConversationSummary {
    let summary: String
    let keyTopics: [String]
    let userPreferences: [String]
}

Tools Details

Weather Tool

  • Uses OpenMeteo API for real-time weather
  • Temperature, humidity, wind speed, conditions
  • Automatic geocoding
  • No API key required

Web Search Tool

  • Uses Search1API keyless endpoint
  • Returns search results with snippets
  • No API key required (free tier limits)

Contacts Tool

  • Search system contacts
  • Natural language queries
  • Requires contacts permission

Calendar Tool

  • Create and manage events
  • Timezone and locale aware
  • Supports relative dates ("today", "tomorrow")
  • Requires calendar permission

Reminders Tool

  • Create reminders with AI
  • Priority levels: None, Low, Medium, High
  • Due dates and notes
  • Requires reminders permission

Location Tool

  • Current location information
  • Geocoding support
  • Requires location permission

Health Tool

  • HealthKit integration
  • Query health metrics
  • AI-powered insights
  • Requires HealthKit permission

Music Tool

  • Apple Music search
  • Songs, artists, albums
  • Requires Apple Music subscription
  • Requires music permission

Web Metadata Tool

  • Extract webpage metadata
  • Generate social media summaries
  • Platform-specific formatting
  • No API key required

Multilingual Support

The app works in 10 languages:

  • English
  • German
  • Spanish
  • French
  • Italian
  • Japanese
  • Korean
  • Portuguese (Brazil)
  • Chinese (Simplified)
  • Chinese (Traditional)

Language detection and code-switching examples are included in the Integrations section.

Permissions

The app may request the following permissions depending on which features you use:

  • Microphone (for voice input)
  • Speech Recognition
  • Contacts
  • Calendar
  • Reminders
  • Location
  • HealthKit
  • Apple Music

All permissions are requested at the appropriate time and can be managed in Settings.

Contributing

Contributions are welcome! Please feel free to submit a pull request.

License

This project is licensed under the MIT License - see the LICENSE file for details.
